_meta.description: DROP THIS FILE into data/injected/ during the demo to simulate a surprise overnight P1 incident. The event poller (Dev 4) watches this directory and triggers re-prioritization when a new file appears.
_meta.demo_instruction: Drop this file at the moment the presenter says 'Let's simulate a new P1 arriving mid-day'. The agent should detect it, re-rank the task list, and push a proactive alert within 10 seconds.

incident.short_description: CRITICAL: Production payment processing DOWN — 100% transaction failure rate
incident.description: Payment processing endpoint POST /api/v2/payments is returning HTTP 500 for 100% of requests since 2026-06-19 08:47 UTC. Cause: the payment processor (Stripe) rotated their signing key overnight and our webhook signature validation is using the old key. All payment transactions are being rejected. Revenue impact: approximately $12,000 per minute. Stripe has confirmed the new signing key is available in their dashboard. Engineering must update the STRIPE_WEBHOOK_SECRET environment variable and redeploy the payment service immediately.
incident.business_impact: $12,000/minute revenue loss. 100% payment failure rate. All customers affected.
immediate_actions: Retrieve new Stripe webhook signing key from Stripe dashboard (Settings > Webhooks)
Update STRIPE_WEBHOOK_SECRET in AWS Secrets Manager and Kubernetes secret
Trigger rolling redeploy of payment-service (zero-downtime deploy available)
Verify payment processing resumes by testing POST /api/v2/payments with test card
Reconcile any failed payments from the 08:47–09:xx UTC window with Stripe
